prompt_text: Design a marathon training schedule for a beginner.
response_text:
# 16-Week Marathon Training Schedule for Beginners

## Pre-Training Requirements
- Can comfortably run 3-4 miles
- Have proper running shoes
- Medical clearance if over 40 or have health concerns

---

## **WEEKS 1-4: Base Building**

| Day | Workout | Distance |
|-----|---------|----------|
| Monday | Rest or cross-train | — |
| Tuesday | Easy run | 3-4 miles |
| Wednesday | Cross-train (cycling, swimming) | 30 min |
| Thursday | Tempo run | 2 miles easy + 2 miles steady + 1 mile easy |
| Friday | Rest | — |
| Saturday | Long run | 5-6 miles |
| Sunday | Easy run | 3 miles |

---

## **WEEKS 5-8: Building Endurance**

| Day | Workout | Distance |
|-----|---------|----------|
| Monday | Rest or cross-train | — |
| Tuesday | Easy run | 4-5 miles |
| Wednesday | Cross-train | 30-40 min |
| Thursday | Interval training | 8-10 × 400m with recovery jogs |
| Friday | Rest | — |
| Saturday | Long run | 8-10 miles |
| Sunday | Easy run | 3-4 miles |

---

## **WEEKS 9-12: Peak Building**

| Day | Workout | Distance |
|-----|---------|----------|
| Monday | Rest or cross-train | — |
| Tuesday | Easy run | 5-6 miles |
| Wednesday | Cross-train | 40 min |
| Thursday | Tempo run | 2 mi easy + 4 mi steady + 1 mi easy |
| Friday | Rest | — |
| Saturday | Long run | 12-16 miles |
| Sunday | Easy run | 4 miles |

**Peak long run milestones:** Week 9 (12 mi) → Week 10 (13 mi) → Week 11 (15 mi) → Week 12 (16 mi)

---

## **WEEKS 13-15: Taper**

Gradually reduce mileage by 20-30% each week while maintaining intensity.

| Day | Workout |
|-----|---------|
| Monday | Rest |
| Tuesday | Easy run (4 miles) |
| Wednesday | Cross-train (30 min) |
| Thursday | Tempo or intervals (shorter) |
| Friday | Rest |
| Saturday | Long run (10, 8, 6 miles respectively) |
| Sunday | Easy run (3 miles) |

---

## **WEEK 16: Race Week**

| Day | Workout |
|-----|---------|
| Mon-Fri | Easy 2-3 mile runs, rest days |
| Saturday | 2-3 mile shakeout run |
| Sunday | **RACE DAY** |

---

## Key Training Principles

**Pacing**
- Easy runs: conversational pace
- Long runs: 60-90 seconds slower than goal marathon pace
- Tempo: comfortably hard

**Nutrition**
- Eat carbs + protein within 30 min of finishing runs
- Practice fueling strategy during long runs (gels, sports drinks)

**Recovery**
- Sleep 7-9 hours
- Stretch and foam roll
- Rest days are essential

**Safety**
- Increase mileage no more than 10% weekly
- Listen to your body—skip runs if injured
- Replace shoes every 300-500 miles

---

## Race Day Tips
- Start conservatively
- Fuel every 45 minutes
- Stay hydrated
- You've got this! 🏃‍♂️

Would you like specific guidance on nutrition, injury prevention, or pace calculations?
